body:
Last month my mother asked me to help her transfer her pension to my sister Carol.

Not a small amount. Her whole monthly pension. The one that helps cover her medications, her appointments, the things she needs to live in my house.

Carol lives forty minutes away. She calls on Sundays. Sometimes.

I sat next to my mother at the kitchen table and helped her fill out the form. I didn't say anything. She has dementia. She doesn't fully understand what she's asking. She just loves Carol and wanted to do something for her. I know that.

I pressed submit. Handed her phone back. Asked if she wanted tea.

She did.

And while the kettle was boiling I stood at the window and thought: I am losing my mind. Not the way she is. A different way. Quietly. From the inside.

I've been a Christian my whole
title: Which Woman of the Bible Are You? 👉
